user_scenarios.user_goal: Run active Supervisor, Worker, lease, and Reviewer regressions for autonomous continuation, then inspect an isolated Dashboard fixture for reachability, freshness, and current-decision presentation.
prerequisites: The focused tests create their own stopped_budget, action-lease, autonomous Worker, and semantic-lineage fixtures.
The focused active runtime tests and isolated Loop Dashboard fixture can run without live cutover.
steps: Run the active Supervisor continuation regression and verify one stable CREATE_CONTINUATION action is queued without direct execution.
Run the active concurrent lease regression and verify only one lease winner.
Run the active bounded Worker chain and verify hygiene, commit, push, and cleanup remain distinct actions with push evidence.
Run the active Reviewer cadence regression across semantic parent continuations.
Open the isolated Dashboard fixture and inspect Supervisor metrics, decisions, freshness, and run history.
expected_outcomes: Supervisor queues one idempotent continuation action and does not create the next run directly.
Only one concurrent lease claimant wins the existing bounded action.
The Worker chain emits distinct hygiene, commit, push, and cleanup results.
Reviewer cadence becomes due after two semantic parent completions across continuations.
在线服务 uses reachability/tmux evidence and version freshness is displayed separately.
The current decision says 暂无待处理决策 when open_count is zero; stale worktree history remains historical.
failure_signals: Supervisor directly invokes Planner, Generator, or Evaluator instead of queueing a bounded Worker action.
Two executors enter the same run concurrently.
The Dashboard fixture conflates service reachability with version freshness.
A historical worktree decision appears as the current required decision.
The bounded Worker chain lacks push evidence or the Dashboard fixture lacks a target-specific freshness record.
cleanup: Stop isolated fixture services and remove temporary loop artifacts.
